question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 (PE1) removes `update-source Loopback0` from its iBGP neighbor toward
R3 (the primary RR). R2 now sources BGP OPEN from the R2-R3 interface
IP (192.0.0.9). R3 expects 192.0.2.2 (R2's Loopback0). R3 silently
rejects the connection. R2's session to R7 (secondary RR) is still
configured correctly so prefixes still flow via R7 — but R3's view of
R2 goes dark, and any RR-client of R3 that doesn't also peer with R7
would lose R2-originated paths.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2, PE1), update-source Loopback0 was removed from the iBGP neighbor toward R3 (primary RR), so R2 sources its OPEN from the R2-R3 interface IP 192.0.0.9 instead of Loopback0 192.0.2.2.
R3 expects 192.0.2.2 and silently rejects the connection, so the R2<->R3 iBGP session cannot establish; R2's correctly-configured session to R7 (secondary RR) still carries prefixes, but R3 receives no R2-originated paths and any R3-only client loses reachability to R2's prefixes.
Fix: restore update-source Loopback0 on R2's R3 neighbor.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.3.
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.7.
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.2.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R2 (2 instances):
- R2: Changed state to NSR-Ready
- R2: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.3
 no update-source

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.3
 update-source Loopback0

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:26:54.563 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.3 Down - Neighbor local address configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:27:07.608 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.7 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:26:54.567 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.2 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:26:54.565 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:27:07.609 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
